title: Commercial Real Estate Financing: A Borrower's Guide for 2026

blocks.text: Where rates, programs, and lender appetite stand this year — and how to position your next deal before the market shifts again. 2026 is shaping up as a year where preparation, not timing, separates the deals that close from the deals that stall.

With CORRA term rates easing and bond yields holding in a narrow band, lenders have room to compete again. That competition shows up first in spreads on stabilized, income-producing assets — and last in land and speculative construction.
What lenders want to see in 2026

items: Realistic pro formas built on today's rents, not projections
Sponsors with liquidity beyond the equity in the deal
A clear exit: takeout financing, sale, or stabilization plan
Complete files — surveys, environmentals, and appraisals ready at submission

blocks.text: The common thread is certainty. Lenders are saying yes in 2026 — but to files that answer their questions before they ask. That is exactly the standard we underwrite to before your deal ever reaches a credit desk.
